history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed during the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step because the source code imports and uses `org.hibernate.query.criteria.internal.expression.function.CurrentTimestampFunction`, a Hibernate 5 internal API that was removed in Hibernate 6.
history.qwen_rationale: Spring Boot 3.x enforces Hibernate 6, which removed several Hibernate 5 internal packages. Adding `org.openrewrite.hibernate.MigrateToHibernate62` to the same step as the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade will rewrite the Hibernate 5-specific imports and usages to their Hibernate 6-compatible equivalents before the post-recipe compilation, resolving the missing package and symbol errors.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails in upgrade_spring_boot_3_3 because Hibernate 5 internal class CurrentTimestampFunction is missing after Spring Boot 3 upgrades to Hibernate 6.
history.qwen_rationale: The MigrateToHibernate62 recipe was previously bundled with UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3, but running them together likely caused type-resolution issues or recipe ordering conflicts. Splitting MigrateToHibernate62 into a dedicated step before the Spring Boot 3 upgrade ensures it runs against the Hibernate 5 classpath, allowing it to correctly identify and rewrite deprecated Hibernate 5 APIs before the dependency version changes.
